public_substrate_rule: standalone public runtime over public input bundles, validators, tests, command-owned receipts, and redacted lineage

authority_boundary_payload.reader_action: Use this manifest to reject overbroad source authority, release, private-data equivalence, and score/progress claims before interpreting status counts or receipts.
allowed_claims: public runtime roots are constrained to microcosm-substrate
command receipts can evidence bounded local checks
redacted lineage and public input bundles can name source refs without exporting private bodies
overread_guard: Do not treat allowed_roots as proof that every file under the root is public-safe.
Do not treat a passing scan or receipt as complete secret detection.
Do not treat source refs, route counts, evidence counts, or standards counts as release readiness, maturity, or product progress.

synthetic_fixture_policy: fixture bodies are examples, bootstrap data, and negative cases; fixture-only behavior is not product-complete runtime behavior
anti_claim: This boundary manifest is a read-model and receipt ceiling. It does not authorize later organs, publication, hosted public surfaces, source-body projection, complete secret detection, release readiness, private-data equivalence, source mutation, score-based progress, or whole-system correctness.
